zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan de Cadella or Calder of Calder Olifard於1109年出生在蘇格蘭洛包爾凱爾索時，她的父親Sir Alexander de Cadella Thane of Calder10歲，母親Lady Alexander10歲。她於1129年與David Olifard在蘇格蘭洛包爾凱爾索結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世在蘇格蘭。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan de Cadella or Calder of Calder Olifard于1109年出生在苏格兰洛包爾凱爾索时，她的父亲Sir Alexander de Cadella Thane of Calder10岁，母亲Lady Alexander10岁。她于1129年与David Olifard在苏格兰洛包爾凱爾索结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世在苏格兰。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Cadella
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Canio, Domenico, Nicola, Sylvio.
Italian: habitational name from a place called Coddella.
Possibly also an Americanized form of Slovenian Kodela and Kodelja: nickname from kodelja ‘bunch of flax for spinning’.

nameMeanings.name: or
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Jewish (Israeli): from Hebrew or ‘light’, a Hebraicized form of various original Ashkenazic surnames.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 柯, see   Ke   1.
Chinese: Hokkien form of the surname 胡, see   Hu   1.

variants.name: Ke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Heng, Yong, Bin, Song, Yan, Zhi, Cheng, Chih-Ming, Chun-Yen, Fan, Fong, Gang.  Vietnamese Lan, Hai, Hoa, Le Van, Mui, Tuy.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 柯, meaning ‘bough, stalk’ in Chinese: (i) from the first element of the personal name Ke Lu (柯盧), personal name of a prince of the state of Wu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the first element of the placename Ke Ze (柯澤), the name of a fief (located in Shandong province) granted to an official in the state of Qi during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iii) traced back to the Qiang ethnic group in ancient western China and the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China. (iv) adopted as a surname by the Ke Ba (柯拔) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).
Chinese: variant of   Ge  .

nameMeanings.name: Calder
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from any of the places called Calder in Midlothian and Caithness, or Cawdor in Nairnshire.

English: perhaps a habitational name from Calder in Cumbria, named from the river on which it stands (probably a British name, from Welsh caled ‘hard, violent’ + dwfr ‘water, stream’). However, the modern surname in England seems to be of Scottish origin, rather than from the Cumbrian placename.

variants.name: Caulder
meanings: English (Surrey): variant of Scottish   Calder  . This typically Scottish spelling of the name is now found mainly in southeastern England.

variants.name: Calderwood
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from Calderwood in Lanarkshire, named from the Cumbrian river name Calder (see Calder ) + Middle English wode ‘wood’.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
Chinese:
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: Calder
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from any of the places called Calder in Midlothian and Caithness, or Cawdor in Nairnshire.

English: perhaps a habitational name from Calder in Cumbria, named from the river on which it stands (probably a British name, from Welsh caled ‘hard, violent’ + dwfr ‘water, stream’). However, the modern surname in England seems to be of Scottish origin, rather than from the Cumbrian placename.

variants.name: Caulder
meanings: English (Surrey): variant of Scottish   Calder  . This typically Scottish spelling of the name is now found mainly in southeastern England.

variants.name: Calderwood
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from Calderwood in Lanarkshire, named from the Cumbrian river name Calder (see Calder ) + Middle English wode ‘wood’.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: New Testament: ‘God is gracious’. See John .

en.lifeSummary: When Joan de Cadella or Calder of Calder Olifard was born in 1109, in Kelso, Roxburghshire, Scotland, her father, Sir Alexander de Cadella Thane of Calder, was 10 and her mother, Lady Alexander, was 10. She married David Olifard in 1129, in Kelso, Roxburghshire, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 2 daughters. She died in Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan de Cadella or Calder of Calder Olifard est née en 1109, à Kelso, Roxburghshire, Écosse, son père, Sir Alexander de Cadella Thane of Calder, avait 10 ans et sa mère, Lady Alexander, avait 10 ans. Elle a épousé David Olifard en 1129, à Kelso, Roxburghshire, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée à Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan de Cadella or Calder of Calder Olifard 1109 in Kelso, Roxburghshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Alexander de Cadella Thane of Calder 10 und ihre Mutter Lady Alexander 10. Sie hat 1129 in Kelso, Roxburghshire, Scotland David Olifard geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb in Scotland.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de Cadella or Calder of Calder Olifard è nata il nel 1109 nel Kelso, Roxburghshire, Scotland, sua padre, Sir Alexander de Cadella Thane of Calder, aveva 10 anni e sua madre, Lady Alexander, aveva 10 anni. Lei sposato David Olifard nel 1129, nel Kelso, Roxburghshire, Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan de Cadella or Calder of Calder Olifardが1109年にスコットランドロクスブルグhシュアイアケルソで生まれた時，父Sir Alexander de Cadella Thane of Calderは10歳，母Lady Alexanderは10歳であった。1129年にスコットランドロクスブルグhシュアイアケルソでDavid Olifardと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女はスコットランドで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan de Cadella or Calder of Calder Olifard(이)가 1109년 스컷랜드 록스버그h실르 켈소에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Alexander de Cadella Thane of Calder(은)는 10세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Alexander(은)는 10세였다. 그녀는 1129년 스컷랜드 록스버그h실르 켈소에서 David Olifard(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 스컷랜드에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de Cadella or Calder of Calder Olifard nasceu em 1109, em Kelso, Roxburghshire, Scotland, seu pai, Sir Alexander de Cadella Thane of Calder, tinha 10 anos e sua mãe, Lady Alexander, tinha 10 anos. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan de Cadella or Calder of Calder Olifard родилась в 1109 г. в местности келсо, роксбургшир, Шотландия, её отцу по имени Sir Alexander de Cadella Thane of Calder было 10 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Alexander, 10. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени David Olifard в 1129 г. в местности келсо, роксбургшир, Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 2 дочери. Она умерла в местности Шотландия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan de Cadella or Calder of Calder Olifard nació en 1109, en Kelso, Roxburghshire, Scotland, su papá, Sir Alexander de Cadella Thane of Calder, tenía 10 años y su mamá, Lady Alexander, tenía 10 años. Se casó con David Olifard en 1129, en Kelso, Roxburghshire, Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en Scotland.
